item.title: France moves aircraft carrier to Red Sea with eye on Hormuz mission
item.description: France and Britain have been working on a proposal for several weeks that aims to lay the groundwork for safe transit through the ​Strait once the situation stabilises or the conflict is resolved.

item.title: Trump sees swift end to war as Iran reviews US peace proposal
item.description: The two sides remain ⁠at odds over a variety of difficult issues, such as Iran’s nuclear ambitions and its control of the Strait of Hormuz, which before the war handled one-fifth of ​the world’s oil and gas supply.

item.title: “Upcoming budget will include every individual and community”
item.description: “In the past, many groups remained outside the scope of the budget, with no dedicated projects for them. This time, we will bring them under inclusion. We have spoken about the ’democratisation of the economy’ — this is our slogan.”

item.title: Overreliance on single sector is risky for Bangladesh’s economy
item.description: The ADB chief economist cautioned that excessive dependence on a single sector is risky, and any unexpected shock to that sector could deliver a major blow to the economy — something observed in the past.

item.title: BDT 740 billion proposed as block allocation in FY2026–27 development budget
item.description: Of the proposed development budget of BDT 3 trillion, BDT 2.26 trillion has been allocated for development projects, while the remaining BDT 740 billion will be kept as a block allocation for development assistance and social development support to meet special needs.

item.title: Bangladesh secures $1 billion budget support as finance minister meets ADB president
item.description: The ADB has also pledged around $1.4 billion in project assistance, which is also committed for the current fiscal year.

item.title: Trump pauses effort to escort ships in Strait of Hormuz, citing deal progress
item.description: Trump wrote on social media that the two sides have mutually agreed that, while the Blockade will remain in full force and effect, Project Freedom ... will be paused for a short period of time to see whether or not the Agreement can be finalised and signed.

item.title: Gold, silver prices rise in domestic market
item.description: So far this year, BAJUS adjusted gold prices 62 times, raising them on 34 occasions and lowering them 28 times.

item.title: Bangladesh-US trade agreement should be utilised for mutual benefits: Commerce Minister
item.description: The minister said that a ‘win-win’ situation is ensured by taking into account the interests of both sides, adding that there is no need for unnecessary concern over this agreement.

item.title: Healthcare is a fundamental human right, not a privilege: Dr Zubaida
item.description: Dr Zubaida Rahman added that the goal is to build a healthy, more productive, self-reliant, and sovereign nation.

item.title: Six more deaths from measles and its symptoms; 1,445 newly affected
item.description: In total, 317 children have died across the country from measles and its symptoms over the past one and a half months, according to DGHS.

item.title: Advance train ticket sales for Eid journeys to begin on May 13
item.description: Each passenger will be allowed to purchase tickets only once for both advance and return journeys, with a maximum of four tickets per transaction in each case.

item.title: Selling fishes from Kaptai Lake brings BDT 225 million in revenue
item.description: The highest revenue share is now coming from Kachki and Chapila fish, accounting for 75%.

item.title: Home minister urges stronger global support for Rohingya refugees
item.description: The home minister called on the United Nations and relevant agencies to keep the crisis firmly on the global agenda.

item.title: FM begins China visit to deepen ties
item.description: The three-day visit, scheduled for May 5-7, is being made at the invitation of Wang Yi, Member of the Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China Central Committee and Foreign Minister of China.

item.title: Local govt polls likely to begin by year-end: PM’s Adviser
item.description: The adviser said that once the electoral process starts, it may take between 10 months and a maximum of one year to complete all phases.

item.title: PM for conducting performance audits to ensure transparency in public expenditures
item.description: The prime minister highlighted the importance of performance audits to ensure transparency and accountability in spending public funds.

item.title: Growers fear losses as adverse weather threatens mango production
item.description: Drought, pest attacks, and recent storms, rain, and hail have caused significant shedding of young mangoes. The adverse weather forecast due to Super El Niño has compounded the situation.

item.title: Deaths of Sikder family members leave billions in bad loans unresolved
item.description: The late Zainul Haque Sikder was reportedly regarded as a father figure by the deposed prime minister, Sheikh Hasina. The family parlayed this political influence into sole control of the National Bank PLC, a private-sector lender.

item.title: No reason for concern over Bangladesh-US trade deal, says commerce adviser
item.description: The pact, the commerce minister stressed, contained scope for necessary adjustments and revisions, leaving no grounds for excessive alarm.

item.title: How an ocean cruise turned into a hantavirus nightmare
item.description: About 150 people remain stuck on the ship, which had been visiting ​some of the most remote places on Earth.

item.title: Middle East truce in doubt as US and Iran fight for control of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Several merchant ships ​in the Gulf reported explosions or fires on Monday, and an oil port in the United Arab Emirates, which hosts a large U.S. military base, was set ablaze by Iranian missiles.

item.title: Dhaka urges expanded ADB support as energy costs and climate risk mount
item.description: The finance minister lauded ADB’s timely budget support but argued that “counter-cyclical financing” must continue if global risks escalate further.

item.title: Gold prices cut by BDT 2,158 for 22-carat
item.description: BAJUS set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 240,337 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a decrease in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Railway spent nearly twice what they earned in nine months
item.description: Railways ministry data show the government set a revenue target of BDT 31.73 billion for the 2025–26 fiscal year. Actual collections lag far behind that figure.

item.title: Taiwan won’t give in to pressure, president says of Africa trip China tried to stop
item.description: China views democratically governed Taiwan as part of its territory with no right to state-to-state ties, a position Taiwan’s government strongly disputes.

item.title: Blast at fireworks factory in China’s Hunan kills 21, Xi calls for probe, state media says
item.description: Nearly 500 firefighters, rescuers and medical personnel attended the scene, according to the South China Morning Post.

item.title: US and Iran launch new attacks as they wrestle for control of Gulf waters
item.description: The fresh volleys of missiles and drones came after U.S. President Donald Trump launched a new effort to ‌get stranded tankers and other ships through the strait.

item.title: Onion, garlic prices fall in Hili, ginger remains stable
item.description: Traders said the prices of spice products have reduced mainly due to increased supply, and there is no possibility of a price hike ahead of Eid-ul-Adha.

item.title: BCB ad-hoc committee initiates election process
item.description: To oversee the election, the ad-hoc committee has approved the formation of a three-member election commission.

item.title: Flood-affected farmers in haor region to receive BDT 7,500 initially: Agri Minister
item.description: The minister added that the government will provide support for three months, and the amount may be increased depending on the extent of damage.

item.title: No grounds to question ICT operations: Law Minister
item.description: The minister said that in his first formal meeting with the ICT prosecution team, they discussed how to address those problems through legal and administrative processes, and expressed hope that necessary steps will be taken in the future.

item.title: Iran says it turns back US warship from Strait of Hormuz, US official denies missile strike
item.description: Iran’s navy said it had prevented “American-Zionist” warships from entering ​the Strait of Hormuz area by issuing a “swift and decisive warning”.

item.title: Measles outbreak claims 17 children in deadliest single day
item.description: Of the 17 deaths, two were confirmed as measles; the remaining 15 children died while presenting measles-like symptoms.

item.title: PM directs simplifying project approval process to attract investment
item.description: The prime minister said the government is committed to creating an investment-friendly environment and emphasised the need to remove existing complexities in the investment process to achieve this goal.

item.title: Oil jumps by 5 percent after report of US warship being hit by missiles
item.description: Brent crude futures were up $5.52, or 5.1 percent, at $113.69 a barrel by 1025 GMT, having settled ​down $2.23 on Friday.

item.title: Bangladesh Bank to launch OVOP scheme targeting 10 million jobs
item.description: The “One Village One Product (OVOP)” plan proposes a shift from conventional collateral-based lending to value-chain financing that covers production, processing, logistics and export.

item.title: Agri dept says 71% paddy harvesting completed in Netrokona haor region
item.description: Although many farmers have managed to harvest their paddy with great effort, the crops are rotting in piles due to delays in drying and storage.

item.title: Highest spending on buffalo meat despite no approval
item.description: Industry insiders say that Bangladesh does not permit the import of beef, buffalo, or other meat products, except for limited quantities of premium frozen meat for five-star hotels. However, buffalo meat accounted for the highest share at approximately $577.8 million.

item.title: Govt targets doubling tourism GDP contribution: Tourism Minister
item.description: The minister said Bangladesh currently has 1,742 tourism spots spread across the country, indicating that every district has tourism potential.

item.title: US envoy leads Bangladesh team to investment summit in Washington
item.description: The delegation is participating in the SelectUSA Investment Summit being held from May 3 to 6 in Washington, DC.

item.title: Air-flow onion storage system fails to prevent rot in Meherpur
item.description: The technology was distributed in Mujibnagar upazila under an incentive scheme accompanied by official training, but farmers say stored bulbs are rotting within weeks.

item.title: US-led task force tells ships to reroute on first day of new effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Joint Maritime Information Center said Monday that the U.S. had set up an “enhanced security area” south of typical shipping routes and urged mariners to coordinate closely with Omani authorities “due to anticipated high traffic volume.”

item.title: Eight river stations exceed danger levels in northeast Bangladesh
item.description: The Bangladesh Water Development Board said water levels had climbed further in most rivers in the region over the preceding 24 hours, with fresh pressure emerging at several points.

item.title: Bangladesh faces simultaneous slowdown in imports and exports
item.description: Trade analysts argue the EPB’s ten‑month export slide is more than a numerical setback; it reflects the global and domestic pressures bearing down on the economy.

item.title: China robot-hand-building unicorn Linkerbot targets $6 billion valuation
item.description: Linkerbot currently holds over 80 percent ​of the global market share in high-degree-of-freedom (DoF) robotic hands, and plans to scale production “soon” to 10,000 units a month from almost ⁠5,000 currently.

item.title: Indian High Commissioner pays farewell call on FM
item.description: The outgoing high commissioner reiterated his country’s intent to work closely with the people and the Government of Bangladesh to strengthen people-centric cooperation in all domains.

item.title: Three cruise ship passengers die in suspected hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Netherlands-based Oceanwide Expeditions said in a news release it was “managing a serious medical situation” on a polar expedition ship, the MV Hondius.

item.title: ADB unveils $70 billion plan for power grid, digital highway in Asia-Pacific
item.description: ADB said the shift from national-only grids to a networked regional market would mark a structural break in how Asia traded electricity.

item.title: Staff and drug shortages disrupt care at national burn institute
item.description: Approved posts for doctors, nurses and support workers remain heavily vacant, forcing the existing workforce to absorb the extra load. A prolonged shortage of medicines has deepened the strain.

item.title: Body of Bangladeshi PhD student killed in Florida returns to Dhaka
item.description: The 27-year-old was pursuing a PhD in geography, environmental science and policy at the University of South Florida.

item.title: Trump says US to help ships stranded in Strait of Hormuz as tanker hit by projectiles
item.description: Hundreds of ships and as many as 20,000 seafarers have been unable to transit the strait during the conflict, ​according to the International Maritime Organization.

item.title: Monira Sharmin writes to EC seeking halt to gazette on Nusrat before writ hearing
item.description: After hearing the writ petition, if the court directs acceptance of Monira’s nomination paper, voting will be held for the seat, allowing alliance-backed candidates to elect one of the two contenders, officials at the Election Commission said.

item.title: Remittance inflow crosses $3b mark in April
item.description: During this period, remittance receipts reached $3,127 million, marking a 13.6 percent increase year-on-year compared to $2,752 million in the same period last year.

item.title: Over 12.4 million animals available for Eid-ul-Adha: Livestock Minister
item.description: The minister noted that no import will be allowed from outside the country this year, like last year, and that’s why leasing of border hats remained suspended.

item.title: Crackdown intensified to curb crime in Dhaka: DMP
item.description: The acting commissioner said DMP’s goal is to dismantle criminal networks, restore public confidence and make Dhaka a safer city.

item.title: Inside Ukraine’s drive to defeat the dreaded Shahed drone
item.description: The pioneering low-cost, long-range attack drones ‌designed by Iran have indeed become the scourge of Ukraine, with Russia unleashing thousands of them on its enemy every month.

item.title: OPEC+ agrees third oil output quota hike since Hormuz closure
item.description: Seven OPEC+ countries will raise oil output targets by 188,000 barrels per day in June, the third consecutive ​monthly increase, OPEC+ said in a statement after an online meeting.

item.title: Anybody can speak in the current parliament freely: Moyeen Khan
item.description: The minister said that debate among political parties with differing opinions is a normal part of a democratic system.

item.title: Hezbollah pays steep price in battle to reverse its fortunes
item.description: In the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut, more than two dozen freshly dug graves were quickly ​filled with fighters’ bodies in the days after the ceasefire took hold. Simple marble tombstones identify some as commanders, others as fighters.

item.title: Govt officials should have mentality to work in all positions: PM
item.description: The prime minister stated that his special request to DCs is to consider every path in public administration as important and inevitable.

item.title: Dhaka’s burial land crisis puts grave preservation beyond most families
item.description: Urban planners blame severe land scarcity. The two city corporations administer 251 acres of graveyard space across a combined 305.46 square kilometres, just 0.33 percent of the capital’s footprint.

item.title: Measles and its symptoms claim 10 more lives in 24 hours
item.description: During this period, measles’ symptoms were detected in 1,166 more children nationwide, the report said.

item.title: GolfHouse Diplomatic Cup 2026 concludes in Dhaka with diplomats, corporate leaders participating
item.description: Organisers said the event is part of a broader initiative to integrate golf into corporate culture and strengthen engagement between diplomats and business leaders through informal interaction, emphasising networking, discipline and leadership.

item.title: GolfHouse Diplomatic Cup 2026 concludes in Dhaka with diplomats, corporate leaders participating
item.description: Organisers said the event is part of a broader initiative to integrate golf into corporate culture and strengthen engagement between diplomats and business leaders through informal interaction, emphasising networking, discipline and leadership.

item.title: PM opens 4-day DC conference
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman inaugurated the annual DC Conference, where key governance challenges and 1,729 proposals were highlighted.

item.title: Less than 10% of Bangladesh’s Madrasah teachers formally trained
item.description: Stakeholders attribute this crisis primarily to the lack of adequate training centres for Madrasah teachers in the country.

item.title: Capital market financing equals just 6% of Bangladesh’s fixed capital in five decades
item.description: Relative to the size of the economy, no other nation’s capital market supplies so little finance. Adding to the distress is the fact that no new company has entered the market through an initial public offering in more than two years.

item.title: Mexican governor steps aside after US accusations of cartel ties
item.description: Rocha’s decision to step aside came as the U.S. has been exerting increasing pressure on Mexico to step up efforts to root out corruption and official collusion with organized ​crime.

item.title: Americans on a budget mourn loss of low-cost Spirit Airlines
item.description: Spirit abruptly cancelled flights overnight, stranding passengers and staff around the U.S., Caribbean and Latin America, after collapsing under financial pressures that included a sharp rise in fuel costs due to the Iran war.

item.title: Germany says US troop drawdown should spur Europe, but top Republicans worried
item.description: The Pentagon announced the drawdown from Germany on Friday, ‌as a rift over the Iran war and tariff tensions placed further strain on relations between the U.S. and Europe.

item.title: Trump says US could restart Iran strikes ‘if they misbehave’
item.description: Trump has said repeatedly that Iran can never ​have a nuclear weapon and said on Friday he was not satisfied with the latest Iranian proposal, while Iran’s foreign minister said Tehran was ready for diplomacy if the U.S. changes its ​approach.

item.title: Body of Bangladeshi PhD student killed in Tampa leaves for Dhaka
item.description: Representatives from the Consulate General of Bangladesh in Miami, including Consul General Sheheli Sabrin, oversaw the dispatch at the airport.

item.title: Info minister calls for balance between state and media
item.description: The minister argued that the definition of media and journalism had fundamentally shifted, and without balance, it would be difficult to keep pace with society, the economy and civilisation.

item.title: Judiciary gets less funding than state-run broadcaster, law minister says
item.description: The cramped funding envelope covered judges’ salaries and the administrative costs of every court, leaving almost nothing for the quality of justice delivery citizens expected, the minister added.

item.title: Home minister warns officers of action for negligence and misconduct
item.description: Stressing the importance of accountability, the minister said law enforcement must serve citizens with sincerity and fairness at all times.

item.title: PM inaugurates Basia river re-excavation in Sylhet
item.description: Addressing locals afterwards, the prime minister said the work would directly benefit 80,000 farmers and indirectly benefit another 150,000, while lifting crop production along its banks by an estimated 7,000 metric tonnes.

item.title: “Priority must go to dignified livelihood and safety for migrants abroad”
item.description: “We have to put human dignity first. Our human rights infrastructure needs to be strengthened. We’ve got to make sure our workers can earn a dignified living and stay safe while they’re abroad.”

item.title: Govt to start work soon to upgrade road, rail links on Dhaka–Sylhet route: PM
item.description: Noting that the concerned ministry has already taken initiatives to begin the construction work, he said, “I hope, InSha’Allah, it will be completed within two to three years”.

item.title: Mexico City is sinking so quickly, it can be seen from space
item.description: Mexico City has been sinking for more than a century, leaving many monuments and older buildings — like the Metropolitan Cathedral, where construction began in 1573 — visibly tilted to the side.

item.title: Iran standoff could leave Trump worse off than before he went to war
item.description: While there is little doubt that waves of U.S. and Israeli strikes heavily degraded Iran’s military capabilities, many of Trump’s often-shifting war objectives remain unfulfilled.

item.title: PM launches Surma riverfront beautification, flood protection project in Sylhet
item.description: During the event, officials briefed the Prime Minister on the details of the project.

item.title: Five years on, Dhaka’s two city corporations fail to revive a single canal
item.description: Experts note that the canals have merely changed hands and no real work followed. The priority after acquiring ownership was demarcation and restoration. Neither of the city corporations has fully restored a single canal since.

item.title: PM offers ziarat at Hazrat Shahjalal’s shrine
item.description: Earlier, the prime minister landed at Osmani International Airport at 10:05 am on a domestic flight.

item.title: Republican governors pursue new congressional maps after US Supreme Court ruling
item.description: The rapid developments underscored how the court’s decision, which severely weakened the landmark Voting Rights Act passed in 1965, has ​injected a fresh dose of chaos into what had already been a dizzying national fight over redistricting.

item.title: Dhaka to study potential monorail routes under revised transport blueprint
item.description: The scheme is cast as part of efforts to ease the city’s traffic congestion and modernise its mass transit. Monorail is seen as particularly suited to cramped, densely populated neighbourhoods.

item.title: US withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany, US officials say
item.description: The Pentagon said the withdrawal was expected to be completed over the next six to 12 ​months.

item.title: President vows to ensure workers’ rights, dignity and safety
item.description: The President said that workers are the principal architects of the country’s development and the driving force of the economy.

item.title: PM joins workers’ rally at Naya Paltan
item.description: Prime Minister and BNP Chairman Tarique Rahman arrived at 4:15 pm and is due to address the gathering.

item.title: Law minister says forces that opposed Liberation War now legally defined
item.description: He said the definition of a freedom fighter under Section 2(10) encompasses those who fought for independence against the Pakistani military and its adjunct forces.

item.title: Golam Parwar insists Jamaat has moved beyond its 1971 legacy
item.description: Parwar insisted that the Jamaat of the 1971 era and today’s party “are not the same”, citing repeated overhauls of its constitution and policy framework.

item.title: Electric school bus exempt from all import duties, taxes: NBR
item.description: The move is expected to cut the cost of electric buses and accelerate the shift towards sustainable mobility in the education sector.

item.title: 40,590 Bangladeshi pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia
item.description: Subject to moon sighting, the holy Hajj is expected to be held in Saudi Arabia on May 26.

item.title: Govt prioritising workers’ welfare, safety and job creation: Mahdi Amin
item.description: Workers, the adviser said, were the key drivers of economic growth and prosperity, with industry, agriculture and infrastructure all depending on their tireless contributions.

item.title: Fuel crunch slows development works at Mirsharai economic zone
item.description: The fuel shortage has forced contractors to keep heavy machinery idle, delaying work across the site.

item.title: Trump administration says its war in Iran has been ‘terminated’ before 60-day deadline
item.description: The statement furthers an argument laid out by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during testimony in the Senate earlier Thursday, when he said the ceasefire effectively paused the war.

item.title: Urea imports in limbo after BCIC fails to secure ships
item.description: The agriculture ministry and BCIC are racing to secure sufficient supply ahead of the country’s second-largest rice harvest. Those efforts have stalled, however, because of the Middle East crisis.

item.title: Higher education rises in Bangladesh, but over 84% of jobs remain informal
item.description: In most developing countries, labour migrates from farming into industry and manufacturing. Bangladesh has seen the opposite.

item.title: Biman Bangladesh inks Boeing deal for 14 aircraft
item.description: The airline offered no delivery timetable at the ceremony. Earlier, however, officials told Bonik Barta that Boeing’s proposal envisages the first delivery reaching Bangladesh in November 2031 and the final handover in October 2035.

item.title: Oil prices rise with no sign of end to Iran war
item.description: Oil prices ​have been on the rise since the end of February when the ​U.S. and Israel attacked Iran, resulting in the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and the disruption of shipments of around one-fifth of the world’s oil ​and liquefied natural gas supply.

item.title: UN’s Guterres says money owed by US is ‘non-negotiable’
item.description: The U.S. has said repeatedly it will keep ​pressuring the United Nations to reform after announcing its withdrawal ​from dozens ⁠of U.N. bodies this year and cutting millions of dollars in funding last year.

item.title: US official says Iran war truce ‘terminated’ hostilities for war powers deadline
item.description: Trump faced a deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending it, but the date was most likely to pass without altering the course of ​the war.

item.title: Iran threatens painful response if US renews attacks
item.description: Efforts to resolve the conflict have hit an impasse, with a ceasefire in place since April 8 but Iran still blocking the strait in response to a U.S. naval blockade of Iran’s oil exports, Tehran’s economic lifeline.

item.title: Hundreds of hectares of paddy submerged in Nasirnagar
item.description: This sudden natural disaster has triggered widespread distress among farmers, many of whom are watching their crops sink before their eyes in silence.

item.title: CCGP approves 100,000 MT diesel import
item.description: Considering the urgency of the situation, the committee recommended the purchase through a direct international procurement method to ensure a quick supply.

item.title: Soaring oil prices ease, markets rise as Big Tech sends mixed signals
item.description: U.S. markets ticked higher before the bell with some of the country’s biggest technology companies posting first-quarter earnings this week.

item.title: Indigenous fishes release eggs in Halda
item.description: Fisheries experts and fry-collectors said the mother fish released an ample amount of eggs.

item.title: Rats infest Gaza’s tent camps, biting children and spreading disease
item.description: An October ceasefire between Israel and Hamas has done little to ease the suffering of Palestinians in Gaza where sewage and sanitation systems have been mostly destroyed by Israel and humanitarian aid ​is subject to Israeli restrictions.

item.title: Govt forms 19-member committee to tackle Chattogram waterlogging
item.description: The committee’s mandate covers keeping the city’s canals and drainage channels functional throughout the year, eliminating waterlogging and tightening coordination among the multiple agencies responsible for development and service delivery.

item.title: Bangladesh’s trillion-dollar trilemma
item.description: The three objectives — reserve adequacy, export competitiveness, and a trillion-dollar GDP target — pull Bangladesh Bank in incompatible directions. Only two can be served well at any one time; the third must give way.

item.title: Iran’s North American diaspora split over next steps after Trump’s ceasefire
item.description: The split highlights a long-running debate among Iran’s diaspora over whether foreign military pressure can and should help dismantle Iran’s decades-old clerical leadership given the potential costs to friends and family still in Iran, or whether political change must come from within.

item.title: Liberation war is Bangladesh’s foundation; it’s uncompromising: Nahid Islam tells Parliament
item.description: The opposition chief whip argued that instead of prioritising the core ideals of the Liberation War, the Constitution placed greater emphasis on Bengali nationalism and secularism, which had been subjects of political debate in the past.

item.title: Commerce minister calls for greater Danish investment in Bangladesh
item.description: The minister invited Danish businesses to explore opportunities in renewable energy, infrastructure and industrial sectors, expressing optimism about deepening economic engagement.

item.title: Bail granted to former mayor Selina Hayat Ivy in two cases
item.description: Ivy’s lawyer, Motahar Hossain Saju, confirmed the bail and the upcoming appeal hearing.

item.title: Man, machine and AI combine in battle to clear Ukraine of mines
item.description: The ⁠unmanned excavator is one way in which demining groups are clearing vast areas of contaminated land more quickly and safely, in a conflict where automation, drones and artificial intelligence are revolutionising warfare.

item.title: Every child to be vaccinated against measles within a week, says health minister
item.description: The minister said the government had originally planned to begin the countrywide rollout on May 5, but advanced it by a fortnight

item.title: Convicted former Harvard scientist rebuilds brain computer lab in China
item.description: Charles Lieber, 67, is among the world’s leading researchers in brain-computer interfaces. The technology has shown promise in treating conditions such as ALS and restoring movement in paralyzed patients.

item.title: US seeks international help to reopen Strait of Hormuz as crude prices surge
item.description: Two months into the war that started with U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran, the vital sea channel remains closed, choking off 20 percent of the world’s supplies ‌of oil and gas.

item.title: Bangladesh hosts the world’s largest Qurbani–centric economy
item.description: The economy centred around Qurbani in Bangladesh is worth approximately $5.7 billion, equivalent to over BDT 700 billion (calculated at an exchange rate of BDT 123 per dollar).

item.title: Two separate bills on BSEC, IDRA passed in Parliament
item.description: By the passage of the two bills, the government will be able to appoint chairmen of any age in the two regulatory bodies — BSEC and IDRA.

item.title: Law minister says 1,855 cases filed over killings and torture under ousted regime
item.description: Charge sheets have been submitted in 158 cases (48 murder and 110 others) while investigations continue in the remaining 1,697, Asaduzzaman said.

item.title: Dhaka, EU seek stronger ties, broader cooperation
item.description: Both sides expressed keen interest in further deepening engagement and expanding cooperation across multiple sectors.

item.title: PM, Buddhist community leaders exchange greetings
item.description: They discussed the government’s steps to maintain communal harmony and foster socio-economic development for the Buddhist community.

item.title: Daily murder cases neared ten in February and March, police data show
item.description: Murder cases across Bangladesh averaged nearly ten a day in February and March, according to data from police headquarters. Police attribute the brutality to drug trafficking, asserting dominance, political feuds and family conflict.

item.title: Chandpur fishermen ready to return to Meghna as two-month fishing ban ends tonight
item.description: Fishermen raced through last-minute repairs to boats, nets and engines, and loaded trawlers with supplies ahead of the midnight departure.

item.title: Gold prices fall again citing decrease in pure gold
item.description: BAJUS set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 240,337 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a decrease in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Bangladesh Railway’s flawed model stalls freight growth
item.description: Railway officials attribute the steady decline in freight income to a shortage of locomotives and rolling stock. But transport experts say the real problem is a business model that sidelines freight itself.

item.title: Initial Australian inquiry into Bondi Beach shooting calls for counter-terrorism reforms
item.description: While the ​report did not propose urgent changes, it outlined recommendations to strengthen Australia’s ⁠counter-terrorism capabilities, Albanese told reporters.

item.title: Taiwan tops Beijing’s agenda for Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Taipei will be watching for any sign that Trump, who has unnerved partners with his transactional approach to alliances, could soften or reframe longstanding U.S. policy on Taiwan in return for China buying U.S. aircraft or farm goods and easing economic pressures.

item.title: US war in Iran has cost $25 billion so far, says Pentagon official
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told lawmakers that the cost was justified given the U.S. goal of ensuring Iran will ​not have a nuclear weapon.

item.title: Runa Laila to receive ‘Minar-E-Dilli’ honour
item.description: The honour will be presented to her at the 15th Delhi International Film Festival in Delhi.

item.title: French regulator says most e-commerce platform products it tested breach EU rules
item.description: The Directorate General for Competition, Consumer Affairs and Fraud Control found 75 percent failed to meet EU rules, and 46 percent were ​both non-compliant and dangerous.

item.title: Japanese high-level delegation calls on Prime Minister
item.description: The meeting was held on Wednesday afternoon.

item.title: Khalilur urges global action to contain energy crisis
item.description: The minister stressed the importance of keeping strategic sea lanes open for navigation and global commerce, saying the free flow of trade through maritime routes is essential for economic stability.

item.title: Dhaka, Islamabad seek deeper multidimensional ties
item.description: During the talks, the two sides discussed various issues of mutual interest and reviewed the state of bilateral relations.

item.title: UAE reviewing multilateral ties after OPEC exit but rules out more departures, official says
item.description: The exit of the ⁠UAE — one of the group’s biggest producers — widens a rift between the UAE and its neighbour, Saudi ​Arabia, which is effectively OPEC’s leader.

item.title: Edible oil prices increased
item.description: The minister expressed hope that consumers would consider the global context when assessing the price hike.

item.title: Second T20 abandoned due to rain
item.description: The third and final match of the series is scheduled for May 2 at Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium, starting at 2:00 pm.

item.title: PM outlines 12-point plan to build a clean and green Dhaka
item.description: As part of the plans, the PM said, weekly public awareness programmes are being conducted to increase awareness and citizen engagement, alongside intensified special cleanliness and mosquito control drives.

item.title: PM urges MPs to raise public awareness to tackle waterlogging
item.description: Noting that Shaheed President Ziaur Rahman had created water reservoirs across Bangladesh through canal excavation and also reduced flooding and waterlogging, the premier said the government has to return to the same approach.

item.title: Study on Teesta Master Plan implementation complete, PM tells Parliament
item.description: The prime minister noted that the technical and financial aspects of the feasibility study now face scrutiny at different stages.

item.title: Rising prices push Bangladeshi consumers toward small and mini packs
item.description: Financial hardship is the main reason for the consumer behaviour’s shift. Due to high inflation, real incomes have declined, making people reluctant to spend large sums at once.

item.title: Incentives to be given to promising sectors to diversify exports, says finance minister
item.description: No description available

item.title: Govt to support farmers affected by heavy rainfall, PM tells Parliament
item.description: The prime minister said the government would identify farmers whose crops the heavy rain had damaged and support them for the next three months.

item.title: City drafts first policy to regulate Dhaka’s hawkers, reclaim footpaths
item.description: The draft policy aims to keep footpaths and public spaces freely navigable for pedestrians while simultaneously bringing hawkers under a registration system and carving out designated zones where this marginalised group can trade.

item.title: British envoy Sarah Cook meets defence adviser
item.description: Both sides discussed various issues of bilateral interest, including strengthening existing military ties between the two countries.

item.title: Government struggles to build a meritocratic bureaucracy
item.description: From policymaking to execution, the bureaucracy’s role grew steadily more centralised during the long tenure of the ousted Sheikh Hasina, and the structure proved critical in maintaining political continuity. Analysts say that inherited conditions and culture make change exceptionally difficult for the current elected government.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold prices again citing decrease in pure gold
item.description: BAJUS set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 242,495 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a decrease in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: King Charles promotes US-UK unity in speech to Congress amid Iran tensions
item.description: Charles’ speech came on the second day of a four-day state visit to the U.S. during a tense time in relations between the two countries.

item.title: Iran’s Guards seize wartime power, blunting Supreme Leader’s role
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei remains at the apex of the system, but three people familiar with internal deliberations say his role is largely to legitimize decisions made by his generals rather than issue directives himself.

item.title: UAE leaves OPEC in blow to global oil producers’ group
item.description: In his first public comments since the announcement, UAE Energy Minister Suhail Mohamed al-Mazrouei told Reuters in ​a telephone interview that the decision was taken after examining the country’s energy strategies.

item.title: Rough sea amid adverse weather; fishermen advised to stay in safe shelters
item.description: The weather office has warned that gusty winds with speeds ranging from 45 to 60 km/h may sweep over Khulna, Barishal, and Patuakhali regions.

item.title: Flash flood in Haor region; two rivers flowing above danger level
item.description: As water levels continue to rise in low-lying areas of the Haor region, local authorities and relevant stakeholders have been advised to remain alert.

item.title: Climate migration must be treated as policy priority: Swapon
item.description: The minister said that, alongside natural disasters, political factors have also emerged as a major cause of migration in recent times.

item.title: Over 72,000 income tax returns selected for audit through automated system
item.description: The agency also said that the list of Tax Identification Numbers (TINs) selected for audit under the automated system has been published on NBR’s official website.

item.title: Militancy no longer exists in Bangladesh: Salahuddin
item.description: The home minister said that some extremist groups exist in every country, and there may be some radical or fundamental political forces as well, and these are normal.

item.title: Titas launches crackdown on illegal gas connections
item.description: In separate operations conducted on Monday in different areas, more than a hundred illegal connections were disconnected, pipelines removed, and fines collected.

item.title: UK PM Starmer faces vote on possible parliamentary probe over Mandelson
item.description: Starmer’s centre-left Labour Party has a ​large majority in parliament, which could allow the government to instruct its lawmakers to vote down the launch of an ‌inquiry.

item.title: SpaceX ties Musk compensation to Mars colonization goal
item.description: The lofty rewards dangled for Musk by SpaceX show the challenge of holding the attention of the serial entrepreneur as he prepares to take the rocket maker public.

item.title: 300 MW power from Rooppur plant in August: PM’s adviser
item.description: Dr Zahed Ur Rahman said the project will meet 10 percent of Bangladesh’s total electricity demand and about 20 million people will benefit.

item.title: Rosatom director general calls on prime minister
item.description: The prime minister appreciated Russia’s technical and technological assistance in the successful implementation of the Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant project.

item.title: Govt adopts specific plans to ensure justice for poor: PM
item.description: The prime minister said that the present government believes access to justice is the right of every citizen, adding that it is not desirable in a democratic state for any citizen to be deprived of access to legal protection or justice due to financial constraints.

item.title: Measles and related symptoms claim 9 lives in 24 hours
item.description: Since March 15, a total of 47 deaths from confirmed measles have been recorded.

item.title: Fuel loading begins at Rooppur nuclear plant

item.title: Lack of coordination among govt agencies fuels congestion, disorder

item.description: Current and former officials at Petrobangla and Bapex do not contest the resource estimates. They say successive governments’ policy drift, weak investment appetite, and shortages of skilled personnel and world-class technology prevented a serious evaluation of those large-scale forecasts, even as domestic production declined.

item.title: King Charles to promote British-American unity in rare speech to Congress
item.description: The king’s speech is expected to last about 20 minutes, a palace source said, and touch on issues including NATO, the Middle East and Ukraine.

item.title: Germany’s Merz says Iran is humiliating US as talks stall
item.description: Merz said it was evident the Strait of Hormuz had been at least partially mined. “We have offered, also as Europeans, ⁠to send ​German minesweepers to clear the strait, which has obviously ​been mined in part,” he said.

item.title: Fuel loading starts at Rooppur nuclear power plant today
item.description: With fuel loaded into Unit 1 of the Rooppur nuclear power plant, the unit is expected to begin feeding 300 MW of electricity to the national grid on a trial basis by mid-August.

item.description: Iran’s latest proposal would set aside discussion of Iran’s nuclear program until the war is ended and disputes over shipping from the Gulf are resolved.

item.title: Indonesia train crash toll rises as rescuers complete evacuation
item.description: The collision between a commuter train and a long-distance train happened late on Monday in Bekasi, just outside Jakarta.

item.title: High Court orders EC to accept Nusrat Tabassum’s nomination paper
item.description: Nusrat Tabassum filed a writ petition with the High Court challenging the decision to cancel her nomination.

item.title: Iran war disrupts the circuit board supply chain, raises costs for tech firms
item.description: The disruption is a fresh blow to electronics manufacturers, which are already grappling with soaring memory chip costs and highlights the broadening impact of the ​Iran war that has wreaked havoc on supply chains, plastics, and oil supplies.

item.description: During the meeting, they discussed in detail various aspects of bilateral cooperation and development priorities.

item.description: Urban planners warn that the revised scope undermines the project’s core objective of restoring biodiversity and improving the environment of the canals.

item.description: The minister assured that the fiscal plan for the coming year would include all necessary provisions to support and expand the programme.

item.title: Local elections may take a year to complete, minister tells parliament
item.description: The election timetable depends on a set of administrative and logistical preparations: updating electoral rolls, procuring and distributing materials, setting and refurbishing polling stations, recruiting and training election officers, and calibrating around religious festivals, public examinations and weather conditions.

item.description: He said the country’s power outlook would brighten and the current level of outages would diminish.

item.description: In terms of Bangladesh’s heavy reliance on imports, analysts are urging a stronger emphasis on domestic production to ensure a stable supply.

item.description: The prime minister formally inaugurated the re-excavation programme by cutting soil with a spade at 11:55 am.

item.title: Mali’s defence minister reported dead in major weekend assault
item.description: A relative of Camara’s told Reuters he had been killed, while a Malian journalist known to be a brother-in-law of the defence minister posted about ​his death on Facebook.

item.title: Trump portrays shooting as proof of his presidency’s power
item.description: Trump, who is mired in the lowest approval ratings of his term after the start of the U.S.-Israeli war ​against Iran that is broadly unpopular with Americans, has experience at the opportunities presented by such moments.

item.title: Case filed over recovery of DU student’s body; teacher arrested
item.description: Police stated they recovered a note and a mobile phone from the scene. The note reportedly mentioned the names of teacher Sudip Chakroborty and a fellow student.

item.title: Measles and measles-like symptoms claim eight more lives
item.description: In total, 43 children have died from confirmed measles, while 216 children have died with measles-like symptoms during the same period, the report states that from March 15 to date.

item.title: Iran war hits Asia’s polyester suppliers to global fast fashion
item.description: The pressure could ⁠eventually move downstream to retailers that rely on Asia’s polyester-heavy supply chains, though retailers are shielded from immediate pain by forward buying.

item.title: Swapon for collectively bringing digital platforms under regulations
item.description: The information minister stated that bringing digital platforms under a set of regulations and discipline isn’t possible for any single country in the present world, and that’s why all countries have to come forward collectively.

item.description: The education minister stated that universities shouldn’t be just a means of awarding degrees but should be developed as centres of excellence, and special emphasis should be given to research.

item.description: Agriculture officials say the combination of small upfront cost and high margin has made sunflower a rewarding cash crop.

item.description: The negotiations were meant to follow historic face-to-face talks earlier this month between the U.S., led by Vice President JD Vance, and Iran, led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf.

item.title: Family demands swift justice for Bangladeshi student killed in Florida
item.description: Bristy (27) was a chemical engineering doctoral candidate at the University of South Florida and went missing on April 16.

item.title: 34,941 Bangladeshi pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia
item.description: A total of 78,500 people from Bangladesh have been allowed to perform Hajj this year. 660 agencies are handling Hajj operations under private management, including 30 lead agencies and 630 coordinating agencies.

item.description: Mahdi Amin, who is also the spokesperson of the Prime Minister’s Office, made the remarks in a post shared on his verified Facebook page.

item.title: Ukraine marks 40th anniversary of Chornobyl disaster under cloud of war
item.description: Millions were exposed to radiation, hundreds of thousands forced to flee, and wide swathes of land contaminated after the accidental explosion and resulting meltdown inside reactor four at the Soviet-built plant in the early hours of April 26, 1986.

item.description: The case of Constable Shakil Hasan carries implications that reach far beyond a single disciplinary breach, crime analysts have warned; it is a red alert for the national security framework itself.

item.description: Israel and Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah have continued to ​clash in southern Lebanon, where Israel has kept soldiers in the self-declared buffer ​zone.

item.title: US-Iran peace hopes fade as Trump scraps talks
item.description: The deadlock ​leaves the world’s biggest economy and a major oil power locked in a confrontation that ⁠has already pushed energy prices to multi-year highs, stoked inflation and darkened global growth prospects.

item.description: Closed-circuit TV ⁠footage released by Trump on Truth Social showed someone running rapidly through a security checkpoint, momentarily catching security personnel off-guard before they quickly drew their weapons.

item.description: Some Republican insiders are quick to point out that the midterm elections are months away, and that much can change before voters go to the polls.

item.description: Rizwan’s creation of a go-kart recently went viral on social media, which also drew the attention of Prime Minister Tarique Rahman.

item.title: El Nino set to return as early as May, UN weather agency says
item.description: A clear shift has been observed ⁠in the Equatorial Pacific, with sea-surface temperatures rising rapidly, ​suggesting a high likelihood of El Nino conditions developing between ​May and July this year.

item.title: US negotiators to go to Islamabad, but Iran says no direct talks
item.description: President Donald Trump’s special envoy Steve Witkoff and son-in-law Jared Kushner are due to depart on Saturday morning for talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi, the White House said.

item.title: Dhaka plans temporary markets on six open fields to clear hawkers from pavements
item.description: Under the plan, hawkers must confine themselves to allotted spots, remove their stalls by a set hour and use trolley-like structures. Neither permanent nor semi-permanent fixtures would be permitted, the administrator said.

item.title: Ending torture requires societal, institutional change: Law Minister
item.description: Acknowledging that change will not happen overnight, the law minister said that in the last two months, no reports of enforced disappearances had been received.

item.description: The official said the remaining 44,978 pilgrims will reach Saudi Arabia gradually on scheduled flights.

item.title: Sugar prices surge despite global fall, traders cite war and fuel
item.description: Traders in Khatunganj say the global turmoil is being capitalised on to lift prices after a prolonged trough. Many had previously bought delivery orders at a loss and now seek to recoup those losses through steeper mark-ups.

item.description: Many producers who accepted orders at lower prices now find themselves exposed. Some are consequently seeking higher prices from buyers.

item.description: BCIC plans to import 200,000 tonnes of urea through private channels before the Aman season begins. It has already called two rounds of tender so far. Supplier interest has been scant however, prompting BCIC to weigh a third round.

item.description: Gazans, who are still struggling to meet their basic needs in the devastated enclave, welcomed the opportunity to vote.

item.description: While the ceasefire has led to a significant reduction in hostilities, Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah have continued to trade blows in southern Lebanon, where Israel has kept soldiers in a self-declared “buffer zone”.

item.description: According to hajj office information, a total of 4,565 pilgrims will perform Hajj under the government management and 73,935 under the private arrangement.

item.title: Meta to start capturing employee mouse movements, keystrokes for AI training data
item.description: The Facebook and Instagram owner has been moving aggressively to ​integrate AI into its workflows and reshape its workforce around the technology, arguing it will make the company operate more efficiently.

item.title: As internal divisions simmer, Lebanese see echoes of civil war
item.description: The ‌latest war that erupted on March 2 between Israel and Lebanese armed group Hezbollah has deepened enmity between the Iran-backed group and its domestic opponents, pushing Lebanon’s fragile state and society towards breaking point.

item.title: Record revenue gap forces NBR to chase BDT 720 billion a month in final quarter
item.description: To meet the revised target of BDT 5.03 trillion for FY 2025–26, the NBR must now collect an average of BDT 720 billion each month during the final quarter.

item.title: Transport costs jump 35% after fuel hike, driving food prices higher
item.description: Higher transport costs and scarcer vehicle availability now burden the movement of all agricultural goods. Import, export and industrial freight have also grown markedly more expensive.

item.title: Russia to halt Kazakhstan’s oil flows to Germany via Druzhba, sources say
item.description: A halt to Kazakh flows would add more uncertainty to Germany’s fuel supply as ​the Iran war disrupts energy shipments from the Middle East only a few ⁠years after Berlin’s decades-long energy ties with Russia were upended by the war in Ukraine.

item.title: Trump declares Iran ceasefire extension with peace talks in doubt
item.description: Pakistan’s leaders have hosted peace talks in Islamabad to end a war that has killed thousands of people and shaken the global economy.

item.title: Cyber surveillance intensified to prevent question paper leaks, rumours: Education Minister
item.description: The minister stated that if any group attempts to carry out ill-motivated activities using digital platforms, they will be identified immediately and brought under the law.

item.title: NOAB members hold meeting with Prime Minister
item.description: PM Deputy Press Secretary Zahidul Islam Rony confirmed the information.

item.title: NBR revenue shortfall nears BDT 980 billion in first nine months
item.description: In the revised budget for the 2025–26 fiscal year, the government set a target of BDT 5.03 trillion in revenue collection through NBR.

item.title: Three more children die of measles and its symptoms in 24 hours
item.description: In total, 38 children have died from measles since March 15, while 185 children have died with measles-like symptoms, according to DGHS’s latest report.

item.title: With tariffs stalled, Trump’s China policy drifts
item.description: As Trump prepares for his planned May 14-15 visit to China to meet President Xi Jinping, the first such trip by an American president in eight years, critics argue such inconsistencies, coupled with his improvisational dealmaking style, have undermined the U.S. in its competition with Beijing.

item.title: US trade official to visit Dhaka soon, says ambassador
item.description: The ambassador observed that expanding bilateral trade would be mutually beneficial for both nations.

item.title: People must avoid panic buying as fuel stocks remain adequate: Info Adviser
item.description: The adviser stated that the country’s fuel supply chain remains stable, with regular shipments of imported fuel arriving, further noting that demand has risen unusually due to panic buying, putting temporary pressure on distribution channels.

item.title: Bhawal national park boundary breached for waste station, biodiversity at risk
item.description: An on-site inspection on Monday found two excavators operating within the Bhawal National Park boundary just off the main road near Rajendrapur Gate No. 2.

item.title: LNG terminal resumes operation in Moheshkhali after repairs
item.description: The Excelerate Energy-operated terminal stopped gas supply at around 6:00 am due to a technical malfunction. After repairs, it resumed supplying gas at around 12:00 pm.

item.title: BNP nominates 36 candidates for reserved women’s seats in Parliament
item.description: BNP began interviews for the reserved women’s seat candidates on April 17, conducted by members of the party’s nomination board. More than 700 applications were submitted at the party’s Nayapaltan office.

item.title: Bangladesh urges focus on global peace, women’s safety initiatives
item.description: The state minister for foreign affairs said Bangladesh, under the leadership of Prime Minister Tarique Rahman, is advancing on a new democratic trajectory aimed at building an equitable and prosperous state

item.title: Four of same family found with throats slit in Naogaon
item.description: The victims suffered fatal throat wounds in an attack that occurred overnight in Bahadurpur village, Niamatpur sub-district.

item.title: Gulf worries US-Iran talks may cement Tehran’s ‘golden’ grip on Hormuz
item.description: Officials ​and analysts expect the next round of negotiations, due in Islamabad, will focus increasingly not on Iran’s missiles or regional proxies but on uranium enrichment limits and how to handle Iran’s leverage over the Strait, the world’s most ‌critical oil shipping route.

item.title: Apple turns to hardware veteran Ternus as CEO to succeed Cook in AI age
item.description: Tim Cook, a ‌supply-chain genius who boosted Apple’s market value by $3.6 trillion in his 15 years at the helm, will stay on as executive chairman when Ternus takes over on September 1, Apple said in a statement.

item.title: US positive on Iran deal but talks still uncertain as ceasefire end nears
item.description: A Pakistani source involved in the discussions said there was momentum for talks to recommence on Wednesday and Trump could attend in person, or virtually, if a ​deal were to be signed.

item.title: SSC, equivalent exams begin with 1,857,344 candidates
item.description: Dhaka board accounts for the largest concentration of candidates, with 366,650 registered students.

item.title: Bangladesh, EU initial Partnership Cooperation Agreement
item.description: Both sides underscored the need to deepen cooperation in trade and investment, migration and mobility, regional collaboration and humanitarian affairs.

item.title: Fate of Iran peace talks uncertain as deadline approaches for end of ceasefire
item.description: The two-week ceasefire in a conflict that has killed thousands and roiled the global economy, particularly energy markets, is set to expire this week.

item.title: Canadian High Commissioner calls on Agri Minister
item.description: He stated that Bangladesh has strong potential in organic agriculture, and plans have been taken to promote export-oriented agricultural production in char areas.

item.title: PM announces Bogura as country’s 13th city corporation
item.description: The prime minister said that now Bogura must be transformed into a model city; this can’t be achieved by the government alone, and everyone must cooperate.

item.title: Army Chief on official visit to Malaysia
item.description: During the visit, the Army Chief also plans to hold bilateral meetings with relevant Malaysian counterparts.

item.title: Government committed to establishing the rule of law: Home Minister
item.description: Regarding the Abdul Hannan Masud claim of being attacked, the home minister said that if the MP has not yet filed a General Diary (GD) or a case with the relevant police station, he may still do so, assuring that the government will then take appropriate legal action in accordance with the law.

item.title: Mobile network may be disrupted due to power and fuel crisis
item.description: AMTOB called for an urgent action to prevent a nationwide mobile network collapse and requested a high-level coordination meeting involving the power division, energy division, Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation, law enforcement agencies, and mobile operators.

item.title: Accountable, democratic govt established through people’s direct votes: PM
item.description: The prime minister said that it is now the demand of the time to ensure that no autocrat or subservient group can ever again snatch away the rights of the people and establish fascist rule in the country.

item.title: Samsung SDI signs first EV battery supply deal with Mercedes-Benz
item.description: The batteries will feature high-nickel ​NCM (nickel, cobalt, and ​manganese) chemistry, powering Mercedes-Benz’s next ‌generation ⁠EVs, Samsung SDI said in a statement.

item.title: Bulgaria’s Kremlin-friendly ex-president set for landslide election win
item.description: Progressive Bulgaria Party’s tally put it far ahead of the pro-European We Continue the Change-Democratic Bulgaria (PP-DB) coalition with ⁠14.2 percent, and the long-dominant GERB party, led by former Prime Minister Boyko Borissov, at 13 percent.

item.title: FM urges Iran to ensure Bangladeshi vessel’s safe passage
item.description: The Iranian deputy foreign minister followed up on the matter immediately and informed Rahman that, despite the tense situation, necessary directives had been issued to the concerned authorities in Iran.

item.title: World weighs fate of Mideast ceasefire after US seizes Iranian cargo ship
item.description: Efforts to build a more lasting peace in the region likewise appeared to be on shaky ground, as Iran said it would not participate in a second round of negotiations that the U.S. had hoped to kick off before the ceasefire expires on Tuesday.

item.title: Existing gas reserves can meet demand for 12 years: Energy Minister
item.description: The minister stated that the country’s total recoverable gas reserve currently stands at 29.74 trillion cubic feet (TCF).

item.title: Process to form 10th wage board for journalists to begin soon: Info Minister
item.description: The minister said that the government has undertaken initiatives to ensure the protection, capacity building, support, dignity, and a safe working environment for journalists.

item.title: Foreign minister underscores SAARC revival, regional peace, global role
item.description: The foreign minister noted that intra-regional trade accounts for only around five percent despite the region hosting nearly a quarter of the world’s population.

item.title: Four more children die from measles and its symptoms in 24 hours
item.description: In total, 36 children have died from measles since March 15, while 181 children have died from measles-related symptoms.

item.title: Price of 12kg LPG cylinder increased by BDT 212
item.description: This marks the second increase in the price of privately supplied LPG within a single month at the consumer level.

item.title: Stable fuel supply can limit inflationary pressure, says finance minister
item.description: The minister defended the overnight increase in diesel, octane, petrol, and kerosene prices as a necessary act of fiscal management.

item.title: A large share of Bangladesh’s fixed capital remains idle in economy
item.description: Although total fixed capital formation exceeds 30 percent of GDP, it is not translating into proportionate economic growth.

item.title: “Strong possibility of a food crisis if Hormuz issue not resolved”
item.description: “Thirty-four percent of the world’s fertilisers come through the Strait of Hormuz. Due to the blockage, there is a strong possibility of a food crisis in the coming months if this issue is not resolved.”

item.title: Energy minister says fuel price hike was unavoidable
item.description: The new rates, with increased retail prices for diesel, octane, petrol, and kerosene by as much as BDT 20 per litre, took effect early Sunday.

item.title: Tens of thousands gather outside Angolan capital for Pope Leo Mass
item.description: Some Angolans had arrived in the early morning in hot, humid conditions ⁠to ensure they could see the pontiff in person.

item.title: We can bounce back, says Shariful Islam
item.description: Pace bowler Shariful Islam, who returned figures of two for 27 from his 10 overs in the first match, insisted the side can bounce back.

item.title: Info minister urges expatriates to contribute actively to Bangladesh’s development
item.description: The minister said Bangladeshi expatriates are invaluable assets of the country as their knowledge, talent and hard work are strengthening the country’s economy.

item.title: Country’s sole DAP fertiliser factory closes amid ammonia and gas crisis
item.description: The factory had been running on stored ammonia since March 4, when Chittagong Urea Fertilizer Ltd and Karnaphuli Fertilizer Company Ltd — the plant’s only feedstock sources — suspended production because of inadequate gas supply.

item.title: E-health card rollout set for June, but tech and data security hurdles persist
item.description: Relevant officials confirm that district sadar hospitals and upazila health complexes have yet to receive any clear directive in this regard. Most sites also lack the necessary computers, uninterrupted internet access, and staff skilled in running digital systems. These deficits could block project implementation within the due time.

item.title: Commerce minister urges non-partisan, effective FBCCI
item.description: The minister said the government envisages an FBCCI that functions as a collective platform for all traders and acts as a pragmatic voice in policy formulation.

item.title: Humanoid robots race past humans in Beijing half-marathon, showing rapid advances
item.description: The race’s inaugural edition last year was riddled with ‌mishaps, and most robots were unable to finish. Last year’s champion robot recorded a time of 2 hours 40 minutes, more than double the time of the human winner of the conventional race.

item.title: North Korea fires ballistic missiles again, flexing muscle amid Iran war
item.description: Pyongyang’s intense missile activity ​is meant to display its self-defence capabilities while gaining international leverage, some experts said.

item.title: Trump, Iran cite progress in talks as uncertainty hangs over Strait
item.description: Tehran reversed course on Saturday to reassert control over the strait, again closing the energy choke point ​and adding fresh uncertainty to the war, which the U.S. and Israel launched on February 28.

item.title: Mediation fails in spat over Franco-German fighter jet, Handelsblatt says
item.description: Citing people familiar with the talks, the newspaper said the mediators - one from France and one from ​Germany - would submit separate reports on their efforts.

item.title: Kremlin denies Putin is cut off from bad news after blogger’s criticism goes viral
item.description: Viktoria Bonya, a social media influencer who is well known inside Russia for her appearances ​on reality TV shows, said this week that ​she supported Putin and listed five problems which she ⁠said officials were not briefing him on honestly.

item.title: Beijing set to launch Satellite Town as China’s aerospace industry grows
item.description: Commercial launches now account ​for over 60 percent of all space launches ⁠and a number of companies are ​rushing to go public, Beijing Daily ​said.

item.title: Ukraine strikes two Russian refineries, Baltic Sea port
item.description: Kyiv’s troops have in recent weeks stepped up attacks on Russian oil depots and refineries - key sources of revenue for Moscow’s war ​budget - sometimes targeting sites thousands of kilometres from Ukraine’s borders.

item.title: Defiant message from Iran as vessels attempting to cross Hormuz report gunfire
item.description: Iran then said it was reimposing ​strict military controls on the vital route, the conduit before the war of about a fifth of global oil trade, as the U.S. maintained its blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: No ‘silent expulsion’ in public exams: Edu Minister
item.description: He said if someone is to be punished, it must be done through due process or a transparent procedure.

item.title: Maritime cadets ready to conquer the world: Shipping Minister
item.description: The minister highlighted Bangladesh's commitment to strengthening its position in the global maritime industry by developing skilled manpower.

item.title: Bangladesh, Pakistan FMs discuss Gulf development
item.description: The two ministers also discussed ways to further advance bilateral relations between the two countries.

item.title: HSC, equivalent exams begins on July 2
item.description: The decision revises an earlier provisional timetable set by the Inter-Education Board Coordination Committee.

item.title: Iran reimposes restrictions on Strait of Hormuz, accusing US of violating deal to reopen it
item.description: Iran’s joint military command said on Saturday that “control of the Strait of Hormuz has returned to its previous state ... under strict management and control of the armed forces.”

item.title: Trump says he has ‘good news’ on Iran as tankers cross Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran reopened the strait, which before the war carried a fifth of the world’s oil trade, following a separate U.S.-brokered ceasefire agreement on Thursday by Israel and Lebanon.

item.title: PM calls measles vaccination failure under past two governments a ‘deadly lapse’
item.description: The prime minister said the government’s healthcare policy rests on the principle that “prevention is better than cure,” a commitment set out in the party’s election manifesto.

item.title: Sustainable solution to Rohingya crisis lies in Rakhine, says foreign minister
item.description: The foreign minister urged the international community to move beyond humanitarian assistance and play a more proactive and result-oriented role in creating favourable conditions in Rakhine for the return of the Rohingyas.

item.title: Teacher skill gap risks ‘one teacher, one tab’ repeating past failures
item.description: Experts caution that without improvements to teacher skills, effective training, regular monitoring and infrastructure, the “One Teacher, One Tab” scheme risks producing the same meagre returns as its predecessors. Many teachers also share this view.

item.title: The Iran war has revealed Trump’s pressure point: the economy
item.description: Even with Iran’s announcement on Friday that it was reopening the Strait of Hormuz ​to shipping, the Middle East crisis has revealed the limits of Trump’s willingness to tolerate domestic economic pain.

item.title: Finance minister hopeful about next IMF loan tranche
item.description: Asked whether the IMF had declined to disburse the tranche, the minister said talks had not reached such a juncture.

item.title: Antiquities smuggling rings re-emerge; 19 rare idols seized in four months
item.description: Interrogations confirm the involvement of overseas buyers and transnational smuggling syndicates, with foreign collectors, private museums and the illicit antiquities market exerting an immense pull.

item.title: Bangladesh turns to US–Angola LNG for May as Middle East supply falters
item.description: Petrobangla sources said the corporation requires 11 LNG cargoes for May. With long-term shipments from Qatar and Oman halted, the state firm has already locked in five cargoes from the spot market as an alternative. Three further cargoes will arrive from Angola and the United States.

item.title: Trump says Israel barred from bombing Lebanon: ‘Enough is enough’
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office did not immediately respond to Trump’s remarks, but Netanyahu said in a statement earlier that Israeli forces remained stationed in southern Lebanon to defend ​against “the near threat.”

item.title: Iran reopens Strait of Hormuz, but says US must end naval blockade
item.description: Trump said a U.S. blockade of ships sailing to Iranian ports, announced after talks with Iran last weekend ended without agreement, would remain until “our ​transaction with Iran is 100 percent complete”.

item.title: Dhaka’s lakes sliding towards death due to toxic waste
item.description: Instead of serving as relief spaces for city dwellers, Dhaka’s lakes have become a public health hazard, increasing the risk of various diseases.

item.title: Power demand surges in summer; load shedding nears 2,000 MW due to production shortfall
item.description: In this situation, while load shedding is relatively lower in urban areas, frequent power outages are being reported in suburban and rural regions.

item.title: Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant's first unit gets commissioning license
item.description: The license has been issued based on the assessment and review of national and international regulatory agencies.

item.title: India’s Modi pushes for more women in bigger parliament in democratic overhaul
item.description: Larger opposition groups said they supported quotas for women, but accused the government of seeking to manipulate the system to get more votes, and said they would oppose the changes when parliament votes on them this week.

item.title: RMCH sees 14 new measles-suspected cases
item.description: To date, a total of 51 children have died from suspected measles at RMCH.

item.title: Apple’s iPhone shipments in China surge 20 percent in Q1, data shows
item.description: China’s two largest smartphone vendors, ​telecoms giant Huawei and Apple, bucked the trend, reporting growth ⁠of 2 percent and 20 percent respectively.

item.title: Trump says Iran war should end ‘soon’, both sides may meet at weekend
item.description: Trump said the next meeting between the United States and Iran could take place over the weekend, and an extension of a two-week ceasefire was possible, but may not be needed as Tehran wanted ‌a deal.

item.title: DUSSD calls for strengthening rights, inclusion of students with disabilities at DU
item.description: DUSSD stressed that institutional backing remains essential to construct a fully inclusive academic setting.

item.title: No liberation for Bangladesh without democracy: Speaker
item.description: The speaker expressed hope that the parliament formed through a neutral election would be capable of moving Bangladesh forward.

item.title: Bangladesh reaffirms commitment to UN peacekeeping
item.description: Bangladesh’s Permanent Representative to the UN, Salahuddin Noman Chowdhury, underscored Bangladesh’s continued commitment to global peacekeeping and its readiness to enhance contributions in close collaboration with the United Nations.

item.title: PM confers Independence Award on 15 individuals, five institutions
item.description: Former three-time Prime Minister Begum Khaleda Zia (posthumous) was given the award for her comprehensive contributions to independence, democracy, women’s education, and nation-building.

item.title: BBS Survey reveals half of women in Bangladesh still outside internet services
item.description: BBS survey shows that 53.4 percent people use the internet. Among them, 56.6 percent are men and 50.2 percent women.

item.title: Italian ambassador calls on cultural affairs minister
item.description: They discussed issues relating to the cultural exchange programme and ways to further strengthen bilateral cooperation in the preservation of archaeological heritage.

item.description: Professor Sayeed met the Prime Minister at his Cabinet Division office in the Bangladesh Secretariat this afternoon.

item.title: Chinese ambassador calls on info minister
item.description: The ambassador expressed China’s willingness to further strengthen relations with Dhaka in the fields of information and communication technology, diplomacy, training, and mass communication.

item.title: Patient load nearly doubles in five years at CMCH, healthcare services under strain
item.description: To bring meaningful change in healthcare, experts argue, not only infrastructure development but also reforms in management and stronger government commitment are crucial.

item.title: Trump tries economic reset as Republicans fret over high gas prices
item.description: Trump is set to use a Thursday stop in Las Vegas to tout his tax and immigration bill ‌that includes campaign promises tailored to court hourly and hospitality workers.

item.title: Russian strikes kill 17 across Ukraine in worst attack this year
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said the night had proven that Russia did not deserve any easing of global policy or lifting of sanctions, with 100 people wounded alongside those killed.

item.title: Death toll rises to 206 children from measles, related symptoms in one month
item.description: Eight more child deaths reported in the past 24 hours as health authorities record 92 new confirmed cases.

item.title: AI ruling prompts warnings from US lawyers: Your chats could be used against you
item.description: These warnings became more urgent after a federal judge in New York ruled this year that the former CEO of a bankrupt financial ​services company could not shield his AI chats from prosecutors pursuing securities fraud charges against him.

item.title: Discriminatory, rapidly changing policies create investment risks for new entrepreneurs
item.description: Without sustainable and predictable policy support, stakeholders warn that investment, employment, and potential export earnings in the sector could be severely affected.

item.title: Govt takes multidimensional steps to dispose pending cases: Law Minister
item.description: The minister said the government remains committed to alleviating the sufferings of justice-seekers and ensuring quick, transparent and affordable justice.
