item.description: Bangladesh’s Permanent Representative to the UN stressed the need for promoting ethical use of emerging technologies with robust data privacy safeguards and urged greater investment in digital skills, particularly for women and youth.

item.title: Kamalapur freight strain deepens amid a two-decade wait for Dhirasram ICD
item.description: The Kamalapur ICD’s limitations are now starkly apparent, with chronic delays and sustained neglect steadily pushing businesses away from rail‑linked logistics.

item.title: Foreign minister leaves Dhaka for three-nation tour
item.description: According to a foreign ministry press release, the minister will first attend the Antalya Diplomacy Forum in Türkiye from April 17 to 19.

item.title: DSCC waste collection stalls as fuel allocation halves
item.description: When a single collection cycle is missed, the backlog doubles overnight. Dhaka residents face an acute waste management crisis as a result.

item.title: Thirty-three domestic commercial banks in India, fifty-two in Bangladesh!
item.description: Observers regard the sheer number of banks in Bangladesh as a structural vulnerability. India’s banking sector over the past two decades has tracked a steady course of improved governance and prosperity. Bangladesh’s banking sector on the other hand has steadily weakened.

item.title: Iran offers proposal allowing ships to exit Oman side of Hormuz free of attack, source says
item.description: The source, who declined to be ​identified due to the sensitivity of the matter, said Iran could be willing to let ships use the other side of the narrow strait ​in Omani waters without any hindrance from Tehran.

item.title: Hopes for Middle East peace grow as Israel discusses Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: Israel’s cabinet met on Wednesday to discuss a possible ceasefire in neighbouring Lebanon, a senior Israeli official said, more than six weeks into its war with Iran-backed Hezbollah. ‌

item.title: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman named in TIME’s 100 Most Influential People list
item.description: The magazine noted that the 2024 ousting of Bangladesh’s autocratic Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina propelled the 57-year-old scion from opposition agitator to national leader in waiting — a destiny he fulfilled in February by winning an electoral landslide after 17 years estranged from his homeland.

item.title: Fire breaks out in multi-storey building in Banani
item.description: The duty officer at the Fire Service and Civil Defence control room confirmed the matter.

item.title: Govt moves to make, implement action plans under 180-day programme: PM
item.description: The prime minister said that in line with the election manifesto, several important programmes have already been taken up on a priority basis, and significant progress has been achieved in implementing those.

item.description: He said reports of the Eastern Refinery’s shutdown had received “serious attention” in the press, adding that the news of two vessels carrying 200,000 tonnes of crude anchoring at the dock should also have been given attention.

item.title: No crisis of petrol, octane in next two months: Spokesperson
item.description: The spokesperson assured that there will be no shortage of diesel, and farmers particularly will not face any difficulties.

item.title: UK High Commissioner calls on IGP
item.description: The UK High Commissioner congratulated the newly appointed IGP and expressed interest in supporting capacity-building initiatives for the Bangladesh Police.

item.title: BDT 11.50 billion allocated for agriculture sector: PM’s Info Adviser
item.description: He said the agriculture cards will be effective in obtaining agricultural loans, adding that the government has allocated BDT 11.50 billion in the agriculture sector from the development budget.

item.title: Prime Minister visits BADC stall at agricultural fair
item.description: The prime minister inquired about diversified agricultural inputs, technologies, and their usage.

item.title: AI-based training to create new opportunities for youth development: State Minister
item.description: The state minister stated the initiative improves training quality and strengthens the link between skills and employment.

item.description: Prime minister outlines alternative markets including Serbia, Greece, and Japan while raising the student visa loan ceiling.

item.title: Bangladeshi expatriate’s body from Bahrain handed over to family
item.description: The minister urged all migrant workers to register through the Bureau of Manpower, Employment and Training before going abroad so they can be included in the government’s official database.

item.title: Trump urges extending foreign surveillance program as some lawmakers push for US privacy protections
item.description: The program expires Monday, and critics want changes, including a requirement for warrants before authorities can access the emails, phone calls or text messages of Americans.

item.title: Pope Leo issues warning on democracy after Trump criticism
item.description: Trump sharply ​criticized Leo as “terrible” on Sunday night, after the pope had emerged ​in recent weeks as a growing critic of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Amazon to buy satellite firm Globalstar in $11.57 billion deal to take on Musk’s Starlink
item.description: Globalstar’s satellite network is designed for reliable, low-data connections ​directly to mobile devices, or Direct-to-Device (D2D).

item.title: Rattled by Trump, US allies eye Japan’s biggest arms opening since WW2
item.description: Despite largely isolating itself from global arms markets since World War Two, Japan spends enough ‌on its own military - $60 billion this year - to sustain a sizeable defence industry capable of manufacturing advanced systems like submarines and fighter jets.

item.title: US shuts down Iran’s maritime trade despite optimism for more talks
item.description: Officials from Pakistan, Iran and the Gulf also said negotiating teams from the U.S. and Iran could return to Pakistan later this week, although ⁠one senior Iranian source said no date had been set.

item.title: Gold rises BDT 2,216 per bhori
item.description: BAJUS set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 250,193 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a rise in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Eastern Refinery limps on ‘dead stock’ as Middle East conflict chokes crude
item.description: The US–Iran conflict has disrupted crude imports from the United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia, starving the plant of feedstock. Its distillation unit has stood idle since Sunday.

item.title: US destroyer interdicts two oil tankers attempting to leave Iran, official says
item.description: The ships had left Chabahar port on the Gulf of Oman and were contacted by the warship via radio communication, the official said. It was unclear whether any further warnings were given.

item.title: Carney vows to tackle cost of living in Canada with new majority government, suspends fuel tax
item.description: The first Liberal majority government in Canada since 2019 ⁠should allow Carney more freedom to pass the legislation he says is needed for the country to ​thrive in “a more divided and dangerous world”.

item.title: Govt introduces Farmers’ Card to make farmers self-reliant, prosperous: PM
item.description: On the occasion of the Pahela Boaishakh, the Prime Minister formally launched the pre-pilot programme in 11 upazilas of 10 districts simultaneously by handing over the Farmers’ Card to 15 marginal farmers in Tangail.

item.title: Tankers pass Strait of Hormuz on first day of US blockade, data shows
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced the blockade on Sunday after weekend peace talks in Islamabad between the U.S. and Iran failed ​to reach a deal.

item.title: US, Iran may resume talks this week despite port blockade
item.description: A source involved in the talks said on Tuesday both countries could return as early as the end of this week, and that a proposal had been shared ​with Washington and Tehran to resend their delegations.

item.title: Nine more children die with measles and related symptoms
item.description: Health authorities report one confirmed measles death and eight others with symptoms as the outbreak spreads across divisions.

item.title: Dhaka welcomes Bengali New Year with dawn chorus, Baishakhi Shobhajatra
item.description: Cultural programmes and processions featured alongside tight security as Dhaka marked the Bengali New Year, while the prime minister highlighted the festival’s role in national identity and renewal.

item.title: Tender for Cox’s Bazar ‘iconic’ station lease fails to attract any bids
item.description: Railway officials said the contracted operator would likely need to invest between BDT 200 million and BDT 250 million to run and maintain the station. Businesses, however, believe this outlay will not yield the expected returns and have pressed for changes to several lease conditions.

item.title: PM inaugurates Farmers’ Card distribution programme
item.description: Following the digital activation, the prime minister distributed cards and saplings to 15 farmers present at the event.

item.title: Climate, illegal fishing gut Bangladesh’s hilsa harvest, down 12% in two years
item.description: Researchers note that rising temperatures in the Bay of Bengal are altering the fish’s migratory routes. Indiscriminate harvesting of females carrying eggs and juveniles, the use of fine-mesh current nets and the destruction of food chains and habitat by industrial waste, plastics and pesticides, and increasing salinity in rivers and coastal zones are together depressing output.

item.title: EU plans more fuel subsidies to tackle Iran war price spikes
item.description: The Commission on Monday proposed changing ​EU state aid rules to allow more public spending for industries hit acutely by fuel price increases, including agriculture, road transport and shipping within Europe.

item.title: Hungarian election winner Magyar vows democratic shift with eye on EU funds
item.description: The two-thirds supermajority that Magyar’s centre-right Tisza party secured in Sunday’s election means it will have a free hand to strengthen the rule of law and potentially unlock billions in European Union funding.

item.title: US begins blockade of Iran’s ports, Tehran threatens retaliation
item.description: Trump said Iran had been in touch on Monday and wanted to make a deal but that he would not sanction any ​agreement allowing Tehran to have a nuclear weapon.

item.title: PM greets nation on Pohela Baishakh
item.description: The prime minister said the Bangla New Year brings new expectations and possibilities, while the rejuvenation of nature, combined with human optimism, creates a vibrant festive atmosphere.

item.title: Brazil backs Bangladesh’s candidate for UNGA presidency
item.description: The meeting featured productive discussions on the existing friendly relations between Bangladesh and Brazil, as well as issues of mutual interest and ways to further strengthen bilateral cooperation.

item.title: Students must register only once: Education Minister
item.description: He instructed the concerned authorities to strictly monitor the issue of re-admission when students move from one class to another.

item.title: Govt starts implementing different welfare programmes for people: PM
item.description: Welcoming the Bengali New Year 1433, PM greeted all the Bengali-speaking people across the world, including the nation, saying that the Pohela Boishakh is a unique symbol of our ethnic history, culture and self-identity.

item.title: BSF detains two Bangladeshis along Kurigram border
item.description: Kurigram-22 Border Guard Bangladesh (BGB) confirmed the incident, stating a flag meeting has been requested to bring back the detained individuals.

item.title: Commerce minister pledges massive deregulation, process simplification to boost economy
item.description: In his speech, Muktadir revealed that the current manual system for obtaining Import Registration Certificates (IRC) and Export Registration Certificates (ERC), which often requires days of waiting, will be completely overhauled.

item.title: Indian high commissioner calls on info minister
item.description: The high commissioner proposed various capacity-building initiatives for journalists and media professionals under the ITEC programme of the Indian government.

item.title: Algerian ambassador calls on state minister for foreign affairs
item.description: The meeting at the foreign affairs ministry reaffirmed the longstanding friendship and growing bilateral relations between Bangladesh and Algeria.

item.title: BBS survey reveals 41.6% of population remains out of internet service
item.description: In total, 99.18 million people have access to the internet, which accounts for 58.4 percent.

item.title: Commerce minister signals no fresh tax burden on businesses in upcoming budget
item.description: The minister added that unlocking private capital requires a supportive investment climate, streamlined business procedures and the dismantling of administrative obstacles.

item.title: Airlines reducing flights on Dhaka routes as fuel prices rise
item.description: Major international carriers are also adjusting their summer schedules to cope with rising operational costs and fluctuating passenger demand.

item.title: Australia appoints woman to lead its army for the first time
item.description: Lieutenant General Susan Coyle, the current chief ‌of joint capabilities, will become chief of army in July, the government said in a statement.

item.title: Strikes on alleged drug boats kill 5, leave 1 survivor in eastern Pacific, US military says
item.description: The attacks on Saturday bring the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 168 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls “narcoterrorists” in early September.

item.title: BSREA urges renewables as daily energy subsidy tops BDT 2 billion
item.description: The association urged the government to slash duties and taxes on renewable equipment, eliminate tariffs on lithium-ion batteries, secure low-interest long-term financing, and expedite stalled solar projects.

item.title: Two metro stations to close for Bengali New Year
item.description: The restrictions coincide with Pohela Baishakh, the first day of the Bengali calendar.

item.title: Telenor Group CEO calls on Prime Minister
item.description: The Telenor chief discussed the company’s interest in developing the telecommunications sector and building digital infrastructure in Bangladesh.

item.title: Wheat imports resume at Hili after India lifts export ban
item.description: Three trucks carrying 116 tonnes of Indian wheat entered the port on Saturday afternoon, marking the first such consignment since September 29, 2022.

item.title: RMCH reports another death with ‘measles-like symptoms’
item.description: The hospital currently has 153 patients under observation with suspected measles.

item.title: Trump attacks Pope Leo, calling him ‘terrible’ for foreign policy and ‘weak’ on crime
item.description: Trump’s broadside against Leo also accused him of being “weak on nuclear weapons,” several days after ​the pope said the U.S. president’s threat to destroy Iranian civilization was “truly unacceptable.”

item.title: Govt faces added subsidy burden; BDT 340 billion more needed for gas and power
item.description: The power and energy divisions warn that the Middle East war will compel them to seek an additional BDT 340 billion in subsidies. Higher import prices for fuel and a parallel rise in generation costs drive the extra requirement.

item.title: Oil jumps 7% to above $100 ahead of US blockade on Iran
item.description: Brent crude futures rose $6.71, or 7.05 percent, to $101.91 a barrel by 0104 GMT after settling 0.75 percent lower on Friday.

item.title: US to blockade Iran after talks fail to yield a deal
item.description: The U.S. Central ​Command said on Sunday that the U.S. blockade, starting at 10 a.m. ET (1400 GMT), would be “enforced impartially against vessels of all nations entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal ​areas, including all Iranian ports on the Arabian Gulf and Gulf of Oman.”

item.title: Hungary’s conservative icon Orban defeated by centre-right opposition
item.description: Viktor Orban’s landslide defeat handed Tisza’s Peter Magyar, 45, a comfortable majority in Hungary’s 199-seat legislature, opening the door for meaningful reforms of a system critics in the European Union said subverted democratic norms.

item.title: Former Law Minister Shafique Ahmed passes away
item.description: His son, Barrister Mahbub Shafique, confirmed the news to the media.

item.title: Parliament passed 97 of 133 ordinances unchanged: Law Minister
item.description: The law minister said the special committee, after thorough examination, submitted its report on the ordinances to National Parliament on April 2. In light of the report, the relevant ordinances were introduced in parliament as bills over the past several days, where they were discussed extensively.

item.title: Furnace oil price raised by BDT 24.59 per litre
item.description: The revised price will take effect from 12.00 am tonight.

item.title: Two ships carrying LPG arrive at Chittagong port, four more on the way
item.description: Experts anticipate that the staggered arrival of these ships will significantly alleviate the LPG and industrial gas shortages in the local market.

item.title: Former Speaker Shirin Sharmin granted bail in attempted murder case
item.description: After hearing both sides, Additional Chief Metropolitan Magistrate Zakir Hossain in Dhaka granted bail on a BDT 50,000 bond.

item.title: Govt promises stakeholder consultations to strengthen key ordinances: Salahuddin Ahmed
item.description: It is an utmost important task for the present government to address the flaws in the ordinance, he said, adding that the law should be revised in a way that guarantees justice for victims.

item.title: EC to publish updated voter list on May 14
item.description: A letter regarding the publication and finalisation of the draft voter list, signed by EC Senior Assistant Secretary Md Nasir Uddin Chowdhury, has been sent to all upazila election offices.

item.title: Asha Bhosle, one of India’s most versatile Bollywood singers, passes away at 92
item.description: She was admitted to the hospital on late Saturday with a chest infection and exhaustion, her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle said in a social media post.

item.title: Hungarians vote in election that could oust Orban, rattle Russia and Europe’s Right
item.description: Opinion polls have shown Orban’s Fidesz party trailing Peter Magyar’s upstart centre-right opposition Tisza party by ​7-9 percentage points, with Tisza at around 38-41 percent. Pollsters predicted record voter turnout of well over 70 percent.

item.title: Farmers to receive BDT 2,500 annual incentive through farmer cards: Agri Minister
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman will formally launch the programme on Pohela Boishakh at Shaheed Maruf Stadium in Tangail, the minister said. On the same day, 20,671 farmers, fishers, livestock rearers, and salt cultivators from 11 upazilas in 10 districts will receive the cards.

item.title: Bangladesh lags in South Asia in minimum-qualified teachers
item.description: Bangladesh ranks last, with 55 percent minimally qualified secondary-level teachers, among South Asian countries, according to the UNESCO report.

item.title: At least 30 dead in stampede at Haiti’s historic Laferriere Citadel
item.description: One of Haiti’s most popular tourist attractions, the fortress was packed with students and visitors on Saturday who had come to participate in the annual celebration of the UNESCO World Heritage site.

item.title: Iran aims to restore majority of refining capability within two months, oil ministry official says
item.description: Deputy ​Minister of Oil Mohammad Sadeq ‌Azimifar ⁠told the Student News Network that repair work had begun ​and ​that ⁠part of the Lavan refinery ​is expected to ​resume ⁠operations within about 10 days.

item.title: China offers incentives to Taiwan following opposition leader’s visit
item.description: A ​mechanism will be established to ease inspection standards for food and fishery products, but that has to be on the political foundation of “opposing Taiwan independence,” Xinhua said.

item.title: Solar slashes irrigation costs in Barisal to a fraction of diesel
item.description: Beneficiary farmers describe a cooperative model in which a committee forms around a contiguous block of 25 acres. BADC then equips the group with a solar-powered pump, an underground drainage network and ten delivery slabs.

item.title: Two newly elected MPs take oath in presence of PM
item.description: Parliament’s speaker, Hafiz Uddin Ahmad (Bir Bikram), administered the oath to the newly elected MPs Mahmudul Haque Rubel from Sherpur-3 and Rezaul Karim Badsha from Bogura-6 constituencies.

item.title: Pilkhana massacre sought to weaken army, says PM
item.description: The prime minister said his government seeks to implement reforms across every sector and to advance the welfare of every citizen.

item.title: Bangladesh sees rise in financial crimes, trafficking and arms smuggling
item.description: The index identifies four primary criminal vectors in Bangladesh: human trafficking, drug trafficking, smuggling and financial crime. The rising scores suggest that although law enforcement possesses nominal capacity, criminal networks continue to adapt their tactics and exploit new technologies to extend their reach.

item.title: Health state minister inaugurates measles-rubella immunisation campaign in Dhaka
item.description: DNCC has assembled a “14-day microplan” as part of a wider month-long push to contain measles outbreaks.

item.title: No hawker policy yet in megacity Dhaka, evictions hit small businesses
item.description: Urban planners caution that indiscriminate eviction without proper planning has never worked before and is unlikely to work now. They argue that hawkers must be acknowledged as part of the urban economy and managed through a defined framework.

item.title: Circling the moon, a Black astronaut inspires millions
item.description: For Butler-Craig, it ​was an affirmation that her path, and the aspirations for millions of Black Americans who were once denied access to the highest echelons ⁠of academic and human achievement because of the color of their skin, are possible.

item.title: Americans weigh in on the Iran war, gas prices and their fears
item.description: Rising costs dominated discussions elsewhere, with a caterer in Indiana struggling to generate enough income to cover higher gasoline costs.

item.title: US leaves Iran peace talks without a deal
item.description: Vance cited shortcomings in the talks and said Iran had chosen not to accept American terms, including ​to not build nuclear weapons.

item.title: Inflation may exceed 10% if war persists, reserves could fall by $6.5 billion
item.description: Bangladesh’s economy is highly sensitive to global fuel prices and exchange rate movements. A sudden rise in international oil prices, combined with currency depreciation, can significantly increase inflationary pressure. It also raises the risk of a decline in foreign exchange reserves.

item.title: Inflation may exceed 10% if war persists, reserves could fall by $6.5 billion
item.description: Bangladesh’s economy is highly sensitive to global fuel prices and exchange rate movements. A sudden rise in international oil prices, combined with currency depreciation, can significantly increase inflationary pressure. It also raises the risk of a decline in foreign exchange reserves.

item.title: New Zealand braces for Cyclone Vaianu, flooding possible for Auckland
item.description: The weather forecast warned that Auckland, a city of 1.7 million people, ​could expect up to 110 mm (4.3 inches) of rain from late Saturday ​to Sunday afternoon, with “heavy swells and coastal inundation likely.”

item.title: OpenAI identifies security issue involving third-party tool, says user data was not accessed
item.description: The company said it is updating its security ⁠certifications, requiring all macOS users to update their OpenAI apps to ​the latest versions to help prevent any risk of someone attempting ​to distribute a fake app.

item.title: Technical education must be prioritised: Education Advisor
item.description: The adviser said the current government is prioritising technical education, and there is no scope for neglecting the sector.

item.title: Iran’s new supreme leader has severe and disfiguring wounds, sources say
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei’s face was disfigured in the attack on the supreme leader’s compound in central Tehran, and he suffered a significant injury to one or both legs, three sources said.

item.title: Dhaka’s 3 bus terminals running at four times capacity; plan to cancel excess route permits
item.description: To address the situation, the government is planning several measures. These include cancelling route permits for excess buses. The initial plan intends to list older and expired vehicles, according to officials.

item.title: Direct farmer-consumer link to ease market pressure: Commerce Adviser
item.description: The initiative is designed to ensure fair prices for farmers and affordable, safe food for consumers by limiting the role of middlemen.

item.title: Trump warns Iran on Hormuz tolls as energy crunch prompts Japan to release more oil
item.description: The Iran war has damaged Gulf energy production, stranded tanker traffic, ‌and boosted oil prices by about 50 percent in the world’s worst energy shock, with Asian buyers among the hardest hit.

item.title: Shippers weigh unusual routes as high air cargo rates, ocean gridlock persist
item.description: The cost to ship air cargo has soared due to strong demand and high jet fuel ⁠prices in the wake of Iran’s continued blockage of the vital Strait of Hormuz shipping corridor.

item.title: World Food Programme warns Lebanon facing food security crisis due to Iran war
item.description: A fragile two-day-old ceasefire has halted the campaign of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iran, but it has not so far calmed a parallel war waged by Israel against Iran’s Hezbollah allies ​in Lebanon.

item.title: RAB intensifies nationwide action to prevent artificial edible oil shortage
item.description: As part of its ongoing duties, the force has recently focused on controlling market instability caused by illegal stockpiling of essential commodities.

item.title: Parliament passes 10 more bills
item.description: The relevant ministers placed and moved the bills and those were later passed by voice votes.

item.title: Govt committed to inclusive, sustainable economy: Finance Minister
item.description: The finance minister said the government assumed office with a strong public mandate and immediately initiated steps to stabilise the economy, restore institutional strength and ensure equitable development for all citizens.

item.title: Ramna maintained with priority as other parks fall into neglect
item.description: In most parks today, filth and rubbish render any notion of sitting or strolling impossible. Ramna Park, set in the ministerial quarter, is the sole exception.

item.title: UK’s Starmer says Iran conflict shows Britain must take a new path
item.description: Starmer said the crisis, which has already pushed up fuel prices in ​Britain and is expected to drive further inflation and economic disruption, had to ⁠become “a line in the sand” for the country.

item.title: US Republicans block bid to rein in Trump Iran war powers
item.description: While Thursday’s action was largely symbolic, top Democrats in the House and Senate have vowed to force war powers votes again when they return from recess next week.

item.title: ADB forecasts modest recovery for Bangladesh with 4% growth in FY26
item.description: Temporary supply chain disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict weighed on activity in the January–March quarter though the ADB expects that drag to gradually fade.

item.title: Exports slip, trade deficit rises to $17bn in FY26’s first eight months
item.description: With exports looking bleak in March, the trade deficit reported in the balance of payments is expected to widen further.

item.title: Energy crunch cuts Bangladesh’s RMG output 20–30%, industry says
item.description: Instability centred on the Strait of Hormuz has driven up global oil prices and throttled supply, inflating production costs inside Bangladesh’s garment factories. Factories also report at least two hours of load shedding during a typical ten-hour workday.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire deal shows strain ahead of talks with oil flows squeezed
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said in a social media post late Thursday that Iran was doing a "very poor job" of allowing oil to go through ​the strait. "That is not the agreement we have!"

item.title: Turnout stands at 51% in Sherpur-3, 42% in Bogura-6
item.description: The Jamaat candidate in this constituency announced a boycott of the election at around 3:00 pm, alleging irregularities and vote rigging.

item.title: Lt Gen Minhazul Alam appointed force commander of UN mission in Cyprus
item.description: He will succeed Mongolian Lieutenant General Erdenebat Batsuuri in the role.

item.title: Anwar Ibrahim invites Tarique Rahman to visit Malaysia
item.description: The two sides held detailed discussions on labour market cooperation and worker recruitment. Both parties reaffirmed their commitment to preventing worker exploitation and ensuring a transparent, fair and efficient recruitment system.

item.title: Vessel with 26,000 tonnes of octane from Malaysia docks at Chittagong port
item.description: To maintain supply stability, the government is continuing fuel imports despite the volatile global market and risks to supply chains, officials said.

item.title: Abu Sayed family voices dissatisfaction with murder verdict
item.description: Their reaction came hours after the International Crimes Tribunal sentenced two former police officers to a death sentence and three others to life imprisonment.

item.title: Measles death toll rises to 23 as outbreak sends hundreds more to hospital
item.description: The latest confirmed death brings the total number of fatalities directly attributed to measles since the outbreak began to 23.

item.title: Pentagon’s ouster of Anthropic opens doors for small AI rivals
item.description: The Pentagon’s growing animosity toward its top AI provider, Anthropic, has opened ​up opportunities for smaller rivals, who have long sought a foot in the door to the most lucrative government contractor in the world.

item.title: National Parliament passes 12 bills
item.description: The relevant ministers or state ministers placed and moved the bills in the House and the Speaker piloted the bills to votes. Later, Members of Parliament (MPs) passed the bills by voice votes.

item.title: Australian parliamentary delegation calls on PM
item.description: The 13-member delegation met the premier at the Prime Minister’s office in the National Parliament Building this afternoon.

item.title: Online-offline classes to be introduced in select Dhaka schools
item.description: The education minister said that several capable institutions have been selected to launch the project from next week.

item.title: CEC vows to deliver elections meeting international standards
item.description: The chief election commissioner stated that they made efforts to ensure these by-elections are even better than the 13th parliamentary elections.

item.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon after huge attacks jeopardise truce
item.description: Israel, which invaded Lebanon last month in parallel with the war on Iran to root out the armed group Hezbollah, Tehran’s ally, says its actions there are ​not covered by the ceasefire announced late on Tuesday by U.S. President Donald Trump.

item.title: No crossfire or disappearances in govt’s 42 days, law minister tells parliament
item.description: The minister said the cabinet remains committed to safeguarding human rights and has adopted an effective stance against grave violations.

item.title: Myanmar’s Min Aung Hlaing appoints new cabinet, military still dominates
item.description: The ​cabinet list approved on Thursday signals a business as usual approach by ​Min Aung Hlaing, with most of his appointees consisting of retired military ⁠officers and holdovers from the previous administration.

item.title: Foreign ministry takes multi-pronged initiatives to implement ‘Bangladesh First’ policy
item.description: The ministry believes the initiatives will further consolidate and strengthen Bangladesh’s position in the international arena.

item.title: Two former policemen get death penalty in Abu Sayed killing case
item.description: Six of the 30 accused are currently behind bars and faced trial in person, while the remaining accused are yet to be arrested and were tried in absentia.

item.title: Millions vote as India begins series of state elections
item.description: State elections do not directly affect the stability of India’s federal government but are closely watched as a test of voter sentiment toward the ruling coalition.

item.title: US Senate to vote on resolution to curb Trump’s Iran war powers
item.description: Trump’s threat that “a whole civilization will die tonight” prompted global concern and ​censure from Democrats, dozens of whom called for Trump’s removal from office. The 1949 Geneva ​Conventions on humanitarian conduct in war prohibit attacks on sites considered essential for civilians.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold and silver prices
item.description: The association set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 247,976 per bhori, citing a decline in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Power sector dues swell past BDT 500 billion
item.description: Experts caution that a liability of this scale threatens electricity supply during the coming summer. Should fuel deliveries to furnace-oil-fired plants falter during peak hours, generation shortfalls and intensified load-shedding would follow.

item.title: First polls under BNP-led government begin in Bogura-6, Sherpur-3
item.description: Voting began at 7:30 am and will continue until 4:30 pm in Bogura–6, which holds a by-election, and in Sherpur–3, where a general election was postponed after the previous candidate died.

item.title: As Trump claims victory, Iran emerges bruised but powerful with leverage over Hormuz
item.description: In the new reality, Tehran has moved from shadowing tankers to effectively dictating terms.

item.title: Iran says peace talks would be ‘unreasonable’ following Israeli strikes
item.description: The warning from Iran’s lead negotiator, parliament speaker Mohammed Bager Qalibaf, laid bare the continued volatility in the region following Tuesday’s ceasefire announcement by President Donald Trump.

item.title: State to appeal against HC verdict on separate SC secretariat: AG
item.description: The attorney general said that the High Court itself mentioned that constitutional questions and interpretations are involved in the judgment. Since the court granted such a certificate, he added, they will file a direct appeal.

item.title: Bangladesh welcomes ceasefire, urges dialogue for lasting peace
item.description: The statement appreciated the diplomatic efforts of all parties that contributed to facilitating the ceasefire.

item.title: Thirteen countries issue joint instructions for Bangladeshi visa applicants
item.description: The statement emphasises that applicants must maintain official procedures for visas, permits, and related services.
